name: Housing Initiative Partnership, Inc.(Hip)
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor and affordable housing developer in Maryland offering HUD-approved housing counseling, homebuyer education, and loan modification assistance.
description_long:
Housing Initiative Partnership, Inc. (HIP) is a Maryland-based non-profit organization with 35+ years of experience in housing and financial stability. Founded to serve Prince George's County and Montgomery County residents, HIP operates offices in Hyattsville, Germantown, and Gaithersburg, and has expanded to Annapolis. The organization combines two core functions: direct counseling services and affordable housing development.

HIP offers HUD-approved housing counseling including homebuyer education workshops, first-time homebuyer orientation, down payment and closing cost assistance, tenant information sessions, and loan modification assistance. They provide financial counseling and community school-based rental assistance programs. Counselors like Zulma provide one-on-one guidance throughout the mortgage process and loan modification procedures. The organization also administers the Montgomery County Home Sharing Program to match compatible housemates.

HIP distinguishes itself through its dual model: they combine free counseling services with active real estate development, creating affordable housing units while simultaneously teaching residents financial literacy. They've developed net zero energy homes in Fairmount Heights, senior communities like Residences at Springbrook, and ongoing projects like The Willows at Forest Drive. They've received recognition including "Community Impact Business of the Year" from the Prince George's County Chamber and awards for affordable housing projects. Multilingual services are available in Amharic, French, and Spanish.

As a genuine non-profit, HIP is best suited for lower-income Maryland residents seeking free or low-cost housing counseling, first-time homebuyers needing education and down payment assistance, homeowners facing loan modification challenges, and renters seeking community school rental assistance. The main caveat is geographic limitation—services are restricted to Prince George's County and Montgomery County Maryland, with recent expansion to Annapolis.
pros: HUD-approved housing counselors with proven track record—testimonials show successful loan modifications and first-time home purchases
Completely free services including virtual homebuyer education workshops and tenant info sessions
One-on-one counselor support throughout entire mortgage and loan modification process, not just initial consultation
Multilingual counseling available in Amharic, French, and Spanish
Down payment and closing cost assistance programs specifically for first-time homebuyers
35+ years of organizational history and community trust in Maryland
Integrated affordable housing development creates practical, real-world housing solutions for clients
cons: Geographic limitation—services only available in Prince George's County, Montgomery County, and Annapolis, Maryland; not accessible to out-of-state consumers
No online credit repair or dispute services; counseling-only model
Limited information on website about specific down payment/closing cost assistance amounts or eligibility requirements
No emergency cash or short-term loan products available
Rental assistance programs limited to families with students in community schools
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Maryland seeking free education, counseling, and down payment assistance
Maryland homeowners facing mortgage payment difficulties or seeking loan modifications
Low-to-moderate income families in Prince George's or Montgomery County needing rental assistance or housing counseling
Renters and homeowners in PEPCO/BGE service areas interested in community solar programs
diagnosis: HIP is best for lower-income Maryland residents in Prince George's, Montgomery counties, and Annapolis seeking completely free, HUD-approved housing counseling and education. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—this organization cannot serve consumers outside their Maryland service areas, and it offers counseling-only solutions rather than emergency loans or credit repair.
services: Down payment and closing cost assistance for first-time buyers
Montgomery County Home Sharing Program matching services
Community solar program information and enrollment
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
